analysis.ambiguity1: "We need to reduce the queue. I want students to order food on their phone while they are walking from class. The kitchen needs a screen that shows orders as they come in." is ambiguity. If all student orders when time up (ex. 11:30) at the same.  The queue is just move from physical to virtual and then physical again for the kitchen.
analysis.ambiguity2: "I need to filter the entire menu" is ambiguity. A product cannot record all the ingredients of food, or sepate similar ingredients, so app is easy to make  a wrong sense of protection.
analysis.conflictSolution:
Interview with Chef: "I need to be able to mark an item as Sold Out instantly."
IT Infrastructure Team: "The cafeteria basement has terrible Wi-Fi. The kitchen display system needs to be able to work offline for at least 10 minutes without crashing if the internet cuts out"
=> With offlinable in 10 minutes, instantly marking SOLD OUT is no way.
=> update wifi-system of kitchen.
analysis.nfr1: Security: App must protect student information, especially allergy information.
analysis.nfr2: Performance: Service must update Sold-Out instansly & push notification at true time.
analysis.nfr3: Usability: App have to filter allergy for students.
